name: hcportal_getEventMessages
description: Retrieves all event messages for a specific event, returning message content with author information formatted for a chat-like interface. Messages are ordered by creation time descending (newest first).

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: The public hasher ID of the user making the request. Validated via CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: Short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN with @publicEventId passed as the kennelId parameter.

parameters.name: publicEventId
parameters.description: The public event ID to retrieve messages for. Used in token validation and to filter messages.

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Used in LOG.GeneralLog message field.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Stored in LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Returns error with errorType 2 (validation) or 3 (auth). Validation checks for NULL/invalid publicHasherId (DATALENGTH != 16), NULL/invalid publicEventId (DATALENGTH != 16), hasher lookup failure, and access token validation.

rowsets.name: EventMessages
rowsets.description: Array of event messages with author information. Messages ordered by createdAt DESC (newest first). Empty rowset if no messages exist for the event.

columns.name: type
columns.description: Always returns the literal string 'text' for all messages

columns.name: roomId
columns.description: The publicEventId parameter value (same for all messages in this result)

columns.name: createdAt
columns.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Computed as DATEDIFF_BIG(MILLISECOND, '1970-01-01 00:00:00', msg.createdAt)

columns.name: author
columns.description: JSON object with author info using JSON_QUERY. Contains: id (PublicHasherId), firstName (DisplayName), imageUrl (Photo). NULL if hasher not found.

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures (errorId, HcVersion, ErrorName, ErrorDescription, ProcName, userId, string_1)
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call after validation (LogSource, Message with IP, Data with geo JSON, Timestamp)

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present (line 22) but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback transactions.
The entire SP has no error handling. If the SELECT query fails (e.g. EventMessage table unavailable), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.
Line 105: LogSource field says 'hcportal_getKennelHashers' but this is hcportal_getEventMessages. Copy-paste error.

Validation blocks (lines 28-96) check @isError flag but continue running subsequent validations. Multiple error rowsets could be returned before the RETURN at line 113. First check sets @isError=1 but remaining validations still run and INSERT to ErrorLog.
Lines 28, 45: Uses DATALENGTH(@publicHasherId) != 16 and DATALENGTH(@publicEventId) != 16 to validate GUIDs. Should use LEN() or just IS NULL check since GUIDs are fixed-width type.

codeSmells.issue: Multiple ErrorLog INSERTs for same error condition
codeSmells.detail: If multiple validations fail (@publicHasherId and @publicEventId both NULL), multiple ErrorLog rows are created but only one error rowset is SELECT'd per validation block. Caller sees multiple error rowsets.

codeSmells.issue: LOG.GeneralLog INSERT happens regardless of error
codeSmells.detail: Lines 98-109: LOG.GeneralLog INSERT runs even when @isError=1, after error rowsets have been returned but before the RETURN. This means failed requests are logged after returning errors to caller.
First error (line 34) logs userId as '<unknown>' string. Second error (line 51) logs userId as @publicHasherId GUID. Third error (line 70) logs userId as @publicHasherId GUID. Should be consistent.
Line 122: Returns literal string 'text' as message type for all messages. No evidence of other message types in EventMessage table schema (MessageType column exists but unused here).

Lines 127-139: JSON_QUERY returns NULL if hasher h is not found via INNER JOIN (though INNER JOIN means this shouldn't happen). The CASE WHEN h.id IS NOT NULL suggests defensive coding but INNER JOIN already filters out NULL hashers.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
Never change the Unix epoch base date (1970-01-01) used for createdAt millisecond conversion
Never change the message ordering (createdAt desc) without frontend coordination
